assumptions.text: Vendor matching logic must execute within the 5-minute notification window regardless of backend load.
Notification channels are pre-configured and available for immediate dispatch upon generation.
Status change events originate exclusively from Workflow execution (WF-2, WF-4, WF-5) rather than direct database updates.
Submission event is a discrete system trigger that initiates the verification and storage sequence.
24-hour visibility window allows for asynchronous processing of background verification tasks.
Mobile devices possess the required camera, GPS, and network permissions to support the capture and upload flow.
Network connectivity is generally available during the field job execution window to meet the 10-second latency target.
The 10-second storage target applies to the system-side commit, not just the network upload time.
Homeowner must have verified ownership of ENT-PROPERTY before accessing the approval UI for this invoice.
Invoice data source must be locked to prevent tampering during the approval window.
Contractor must hold an active license prior to initiating any payment request.
The 3-second SLA applies to end-to-end latency including external gateway response time.
Payment settlement assumes the Work Order (WF-4) is fully marked complete.
Investor identity exists in ENT-USER with a distinct permission set before portfolio data is served.
GL data access is read-only during the Reserve Study review session to prevent race conditions.
Reserve Study documents must be stored in ENT-DOC-FOLDER before being referenced by the review report.
Vote submission requires a signed hash of the motion payload before transmission.
Board member status must be verified via ENT-SESSION-TOKEN during voting session.
Immutable ledger write must be atomic with the vote record creation to prevent partial states.
Staff role requires specific 'Dispute Reviewer' permission set to create tickets for payment disputes.
The 'full context' payload must exclude PII not necessary for resolution to comply with data minimization rules.
Incident status transition is atomic and persists even if the ticket generation fails partway through.
System uses a symmetric key algorithm for file encryption to ensure consistency across storage nodes.
File paths must include property identifier for retrieval to support multi-property homeowner views.
AI inference engine must serve predictions within the 15-second window to meet the overall SLA.
Vendor license verification API calls are available synchronously or via pre-fetched cache.
AI scoring model weights are static during a search session to ensure consistency.
The 'estimated travel distance' baseline is stored as a historical value for each batch of work orders to enable the 10% calculation.
Technician location data is updated synchronously with the route optimization calculation engine.
The compliance check (COMP-4) is required for every route re-calculation event.
Manager role must be explicitly subscribed to compliance notification streams to receive alerts.
License expiration date is stored in a non-leap-year normalized format (YYYY-MM-DD).
Metric calculation latency is acceptable even if `ENT-VENDOR-SCORE` updates occur asynchronously during report generation.
Investor persona access to `ENT-VENDOR-SCORE` implies aggregated visibility across multiple `ENT-PROPERTY` records.
Offline storage size is bounded by device capability limits (e.g., 500MB local cache).
Network reconnection is detected by the mobile client before the sync process begins.
Technician role permissions allow read/write to local store but not direct DB manipulation.
User session token issued by Better-Auth includes a tenant_id claim in the JWT payload.
Tenant isolation must be enforced at the database engine level (RLS) rather than purely in application logic.
Property entities must support a one-to-one or many-to-one mapping to a tenant (property or association) for isolation.
AI Decision Log entries are populated by WF-3 or WF-14 execution paths before the user can view them.
The 'TBD' placeholder is displayed because rationale generation is asynchronous or not yet available for legacy cases.
Trust Ledger UI is a standard component within the SvelteKit frontend infrastructure.
External partner traffic is expected to be unauthenticated until a specific token issuance is triggered.
The placeholder response must be deterministic regardless of the backend load state.
Nextdoor integration does not require immediate database schema changes.
Legacy data import strategy is defined at the system level, not per property entity.
System documentation is stored as versioned content (ENT-DOC-VERSION) rather than a flat text file.
Q-4 reference implies a specific Questionnaire Item or Requirement Document that defines migration parameters.
